title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is about evaluation reliability and controlled access to high-capability cyber models. New work questions whether LLM judges can be gamed by contextual framing, while OpenAI is expanding a vetted-access model for cybersecurity use cases with added safeguards, underscoring a broader shift toward tighter evaluation, deployment, and risk-management practices.

items.title: Context framing can distort LLM-as-a-judge evaluations
items.why_it_matters: It highlights a concrete failure mode in automated evaluation pipelines used to assess model quality and safety.

items.title: OpenAI expands trusted access program for cyber defense with GPT-5.4-Cyber
items.why_it_matters: It shows a governance approach for deploying sensitive capabilities through vetting, access controls, and safeguards.

items.title: OpenAI says security firms and enterprises join Trusted Access for Cyber
items.why_it_matters: It indicates the trusted-access model is moving from policy design into operational cyber defense deployment.

items.title: AD4AD benchmarks visual anomaly detection for safer autonomous driving
items.why_it_matters: It targets out-of-distribution perception failures that can undermine safety in real-world autonomous driving.

items.title: RAD-2 scales generator-discriminator reinforcement learning for autonomous driving
items.why_it_matters: It focuses on robustness under uncertainty and closed-loop interaction in a safety-critical deployment setting.

items.title: CoopEval benchmarks cooperation-sustaining mechanisms for LLM agents
items.why_it_matters: It addresses whether increasingly capable agents remain cooperative in multi-agent settings with conflicting incentives.

items.title: Vision-based human-robot collaboration framework adds uncertainty guarantees
items.why_it_matters: It combines uncertainty estimation and OOD detection to support certifiable safety around humans.

items.title: Agentic Microphysics proposes a safety framework for interacting AI agents
items.why_it_matters: It argues safety analysis must extend beyond single models to population-level interaction risks.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
